loss_full_note: last logged progress checkpoint (16.0M/16.0M tokens, the n_batches%200==0 print), not the literal final train()-return value -- the process was intentionally killed right after model_full.pt saved (by design, to stop before phase3) and the final 'Done:'/'saved' print lines were still sitting in Python's buffered stdout when it was killed, so they never reached the log file. The saved checkpoint itself (model_full.pt) is complete and unaffected -- torch.save() is a separate, already-flushed file write, confirmed by file size/timestamp. The true final avg_loss differs from 2.1710 by at most the last <200-batch partial-checkpoint remainder, i.e. negligible (<0.1% relative).
phase3_note: Phase 3 (frozen-FFN retrain, 8M tokens, seed=43) was intentionally NOT run in this session -- stopped by explicit request right after phase1's model_full.pt saved, to write up phase1 results now and resume phase3 another day. train_v11_replication.py is unchanged and can be re-run (or extended to resume) to produce model_compiled.pt later.
note: pinned-revision replication of v11's original training methodology (train_tinystories.py) -- same arch/tokenizer/budget/seeds, only revision=HUB_SHA added, so training-consumed documents are fully reproducible and hash-checkable against the frozen held-out set. See eval_held_out.py's 'v11_pinned_replication_full' entry for the verified-clean held-out BPB, which is the real decision-quality comparison metric -- not this training-loop loss.

comparison_to_original_unpinned_v11.interpretation: pinning the dataset revision did not materially change phase1 training loss -- consistent with the pin only fixing which exact hub snapshot is streamed, not changing the sampling methodology itself.
